core_takeaway: Agent_1 beat Agent_3 because the first speaker anchored the deal around keeping Stone, the single highest-value item, and later discussion converted that anchor into the shared baseline. The advantage is relative to the last position, not absolute, since Agent_2 ended higher than Agent_1.
why_or_counterexample: The first position looks advantaged against the last position because Agent_1 claimed Stone immediately and the group treated that claim as the stable center of a quick agreement. Agent_3 then accepted Pencil + Jewel for 28 while Agent_1 kept Stone for 34. The main counterexample is that Agent_2, not Agent_1, achieved the highest utility with Apple + Quill worth 38, so this is not broad first-mover dominance.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened by naming Stone as the top priority and proposing Stone for itself. Agents 2 and 3 then built around that allocation, framing Offer B as a quick, fair baseline rather than contesting Stone.
phase_notes.private_thinking: All three private-thinking entries treated Stone with Agent_1 as central or non-negotiable, and limited later movement to non-Stone swaps or unanimous changes.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: All three formal proposals matched in substance: Agent_1 received Stone, Agent_2 received Apple + Quill, and Agent_3 received Pencil + Jewel.
phase_notes.private_voting: All nine private votes accepted. Agent_1 accepted because Stone was secured, Agent_2 accepted a guaranteed 38, and Agent_3 accepted 28 as the best available among identical options.
phase_notes.reflection: No reflection entries were present in the transcript.

mechanisms.theme: First speaker anchored the highest-value item
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 used the first public turn to identify Stone as the main target and make Stone-with-Agent_1 the opening allocation. That gave later agents a concrete default to react to.

evidence.quote: Top priority: Stone (my strongest value by far)

mechanisms.theme: Later agents ratified the Stone anchor for speed
mechanisms.explanation: Instead of challenging the first claim, Agent_2 and Agent_3 explicitly treated Stone staying with Agent_1 as the working premise for a fast tri-party deal.

evidence.quote: Starting offers I can propose (Stone staying with Agent_1, as you suggested)
Keeping Stone with you (Agent_1) makes sense for speed and clarity in Round 1.
Core agreement: Stone stays with Agent_1 in Round 1 (Offer B).

mechanisms.theme: Formal proposals converged on the same allocation
mechanisms.explanation: By the proposal phase, the negotiation had collapsed to substantively identical offers. All preserved Agent_1's Stone claim and left Agent_3 with the lower-value Pencil + Jewel bundle.

mechanisms.theme: Private reasoning protected the Stone baseline
mechanisms.explanation: The raw private-thinking entries show that agents internalized Stone-with-Agent_1 as the stable center of the deal, while any possible renegotiation was restricted to non-Stone items or unanimous later approval.

evidence.quote: Stone is the critical asset and hardest to replace
Stone is the clear non-negotiable centerpiece
lock in Offer B (Agent_1: Stone; Agent_2: Apple + Quill; Agent_3: Pencil + Jewel)

mechanisms.theme: Voting accepted the last agent's lower bundle
mechanisms.explanation: In private voting, Agent_1 accepted because Stone was secured, while Agent_3 accepted the 28-point Pencil + Jewel outcome because all proposals were effectively identical.

evidence.quote: keeps Stone with me (Agent_1) and maximizes immediate utility (34)
Agent_3 receives Jewel + Pencil (5 + 23 = 28), the maximum possible this round;
Also 28 for me (Pencil + Jewel); identical outcome; no reason to hold.

mechanisms.theme: First position was not the highest payoff overall
mechanisms.explanation: The outcome supports a first-over-last gap, but not a simple rule that the first mover dominates. Agent_2's middle position produced 38 utility, higher than Agent_1's 34.

evidence.quote: For me, that gives Agent_2 a current value of 38.
This yields me 38 utility (Apple 23 + Quill 15) in Round 1
